topic: Part 6.13 The MOND Grouping — A Category-Error Import (c3-patches verification)
change_summary: Decider self-applied c3 H2, H3, H5, H6, H7 patches at 2026-05-19T17:48:09Z (file: monitor/decisions/applied-patches/2026-05-19.json). Verifying patches landed cleanly; ISS-2135 (H1 → EXP-428) and ISS-2137 (H4) remain open and out-of-scope for this verification.
our_argument_summary: Section argues the eleven late-March-2026 dome WINs (WIN-044…054 commit burst) split into three categories: (a) five MOND-canonical anomaly imports (WIN-047/048/052/054/055) reclassified Self-Contradicted under "category-error import" sub-rationale; (b) five tidal-constituent items (WIN-045/046/049/050/051) handled as standard physics relabeled as dome predictions; (c) two dome-internal failed derivations (WIN-044/053). Kernel-of-truth concedes the dome author had an internally-coherent ΛCDM-Counter-Prediction sub-category available (WIN-048 carries the label); self-own is the placement of the rest under the strongest-claim "confirmed WINs" tier. Closed-world h3 establishes that the bounded-firmament parameters (disc_radius ≈ 20,015 km; firmament height ≈ 8,537–9,086 km; sun/moon at sub-6,000 km) formally foreclose any disc-cosmology derivation of MOND-scale phenomena, with a third-option rebuttal for the firmament-projection-illusion retreat.

H2_remove_preceding_paragraphs_cite.evidence: Section now reads 'and the dome has produced no derivation from firmament-projection mechanics that would substitute for it on any of these specific signatures (see WIN-052 detail).' Zero occurrences of 'see the preceding paragraphs' remain (data/sections.json:p6-613-mond-grouping closed-world h3).

H3_narrow_WIN029_citation.evidence: Section now reads 'the dome\'s own published documents listing inconsistent firmament-height values — 8,537 km (formula at r=0) and 9,086 km (model parameter average) — a contradiction WIN-029 documents (...; 9,572 km is the Schumann quarter-wave derivation the dome cites to argue for the conductive ceiling, not a separately-inconsistent baseline constant).' Cross-checked against data/wins.json:WIN-029 detail_evidence, which explicitly states two internally inconsistent firmament heights: H(r=0) = 8,537 km from its own exponential formula, vs. firmament_height = 9,086 km — a 6.4% discrepancy never acknowledged. c4 prose now matches WIN-029 verbatim framing.

H5_remove_Triton_Station.evidence: Section contains zero occurrences of 'Triton Station' or 'tritonstation'; Banik & Zhao 2022 (DOI 10.3390/sym14071331) citation stands alone as the canonical MOND-anomaly survey reference. Slight wording softening ('it circulates in the MOND-curious sphere as the canonical set of anomalies') preserves the argument.

H6_remove_69_count_from_kernel_h3.evidence: Kernel h3 now reads '(confirmed WINs, tallied into the "Confirmed Predictions" scorecard on the home page)' — the version-tied '69' was removed here as advertised. HOWEVER, the same string survives later in the section's self-own h3 (paragraph 3 of 'The category placement, and the self-own'): 'contributing to the headline "69 Confirmed Predictions" scorecard on the home page.' The applied-patches log says 'removed version-specific 69 dome WIN count from kernel h3' — accurate scope description, but the section now has asymmetric treatment of the same vulnerability. See new hole H1 below.

H7_remove_plasma_cosmology.evidence: Section contains zero occurrences of 'Plasma cosmology' or 'plasma cosmology'. Deeper-lesson h3 now reads 'MOND commits to galactic-scale 3D physics. Modified-inertia models commit to their own dimensional architectures.' — two-element list, c3 H7 anticipated the rhythm change and judged it acceptable ('The list-of-three rhythm is rhetorical, not load-bearing').

holes_found.description: H6 patch incomplete — same '69 Confirmed Predictions' version-tied citation survives in the self-own h3 paragraph 3. The c3 H6 hole flagged this in the kernel h3 and the decider correctly removed it there, but the identical scare-quoted citation 'contributing to the headline "69 Confirmed Predictions" scorecard on the home page' remains in the self-own h3 (data/sections.json:p6-613-mond-grouping 'The category placement, and the self-own' h3, paragraph 3). The poller as of 2026-05-19T09:10Z still reports win_count='69 CLEAN' on the dome home page, so the citation is factually accurate AT THIS MOMENT — but the underlying H6 rationale was that any specific-count citation will go stale on the dome's next manual commit, and the kernel-h3 fix accepted that rationale. The asymmetry between the two h3s leaves a known-vulnerable surface in the same section. Note: today's decider run already pushed our llms.txt from '69 dome WIN numbers' to '70 dome WIN numbers' (social-draft-accept patch in 2026-05-19.json) because WIN-070 was added — so our internal count diverged from the dome's home count today, increasing the staleness pressure on this specific citation.
holes_found.recommendation: Apply the same fix to the self-own h3 paragraph 3: replace 'contributing to the headline "69 Confirmed Predictions" scorecard on the home page' with 'contributing to the headline confirmed-predictions scorecard on the home page' (generalized form — matches the kernel-h3 patch). One-token diff. No argumentative content lost — the punch in this paragraph is 'WIN-052 is confirmed by RAR, WIN-054 by El Gordo', not the specific count. Defender-resistant version.

holes_found.description: H2 patch produced a redundant WIN-052 citation in the same h3. The closed-world h3 now references '(see WIN-052 detail)' in the third-option paragraph opener AND '(see WIN-052 detail)' at the end of the same h3 (~200 words apart, two anchor occurrences in a single h3). The c3 H2 recommendation preferred Option A — citing the no-derivation-chain argument earlier in this subsection — over Option B (appending a half-sentence about WIN-044/053). The decider picked a third path: remove the wrong half of the original citation ('see the preceding paragraphs and') while keeping the WIN-052 reference. The remaining citation is correct, but it's the same anchor the h3 already invoked one paragraph earlier. A defender clicking the second link lands on the same WIN-052 panel they were sent to the first time. Light reading-flow issue, not load-bearing.
holes_found.recommendation: Two options. Option A (preferred, smaller diff): change the closing parenthetical from '(see WIN-052 detail)' to '(see WIN-052 detail above and the no-derivation-chain argument earlier in this subsection)' — folds in the c3-H2 Option A guidance verbatim, gives the closing citation a substantive target the opening one didn't cover. Option B (smaller still): drop the closing parenthetical entirely; the sentence reads cleanly as 'and the dome has produced no derivation from firmament-projection mechanics that would substitute for it on any of these specific signatures.' Both options are <50-char diffs.

carry_over_notes: c3 H1 (kernel/self-own redundancy, moderate) is tracked as ISS-2135 → routed to EXP-428 (assigned-analyst, open). c3 H4 (kernel 'defensible if differently-framed' vs closed-world 'no framing rescues', minor) is tracked as ISS-2137 (assigned-analyst, open). Neither is restated in this c4 holes_found because both are in-flight in the analyst queue; surfacing them would double-count work the analyst is owning. Both are noted here so the decider's M3 carry-over enforcement sees a deliberate omission rather than a forgotten one. unresolved_prior_cycle is intentionally empty per the rule that it lists only carry-over holes I am RESTATING.

stronger_arguments.description: If we want the section to be entirely robust against future dome version increments, we could prophylactically scan for ANY scare-quoted dome-state phrase in the section ('"X Confirmed Predictions"', '"V51.1"', specific WIN-NNN numerical scorecards, etc.) and either pin them to '(as of YYYY-MM-DD)' or generalize them. The kernel/self-own H6 asymmetry suggests this is a class of issue that should be addressed sectionwide, not hole-by-hole. Low-priority follow-up — not blocking this verification.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome author's published claim taxonomy actually does contain the sub-category 'ΛCDM Counter-Prediction' that the section identifies (data/wins.json:WIN-048 detail_evidence carries the label verbatim). The kernel h3 — inserted by EXP-427 — correctly acknowledges this. The c4 verification does not disturb that acknowledgment; the kernel-of-truth here is well-handled and the H6 partial-patch issue does not undermine it.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The kernel concession does not rescue the WINs because the closed-world h3 (data/sections.json:p6-613-mond-grouping h3 'Why the closed-world commitment forecloses the derivation route') establishes that the dome's bounded-firmament commitments (disc_radius ≈ 20,015 km; firmament heights 8,537–9,086 km per data/wins.json:WIN-029 detail_evidence; sun/moon at 5,733 km / 2,534 km altitudes) mathematically foreclose any disc-cosmology derivation of MOND-scale phenomena — the dimensional architecture simply does not exist in the model. Granting the dome author had an internally-coherent sub-category does not grant that the sub-category contains material capable of being a dome-prediction in any framing. ISS-2137 (open) tracks the residual tension between the kernel concession and the closed-world demolition; that work is owned by the analyst.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Not applicable — section-level review. The verification focused on prose-vs-data consistency: section's WIN-029 framing matches WIN-029 detail_evidence; section's MOND anomaly attribution matches the imported Banik & Zhao 2022 Symmetry citation (DOI 10.3390/sym14071331); section's '69 Confirmed Predictions' headline citation matches the poller-observed dome state but is version-tied.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Section prose is hardcoded narrative — not generated from data. The version-tied citations (e.g. '69 Confirmed Predictions') are the manual-write equivalent of hardcoded data values; same staleness risk.
data_sources_used: data/wins.json (verdicts and detail_evidence for WIN-029, WIN-047/048/052/054/055)
monitor/changes/chg-20260519-0910-001.json (poller parameter canary for current dome state)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None in the section. The third-option rebuttal in the closed-world h3 explicitly closes the firmament-projection-illusion retreat (option A / option B / 'a third reading does not produce an option (C)').
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new. The H6 partial-patch asymmetry is a prose-only issue, not a data discrepancy.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section-level reviews of structural arguments do not exercise monitor.py automation. No per-WIN monitoring tag applies at section scope.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Section attacks the dome author's import of MOND-community anomalies; it does not itself relabel standard physics. The per-WIN reclassifications (WIN-047/048/052/054/055 → Self-Contradicted) ARE the section's analytical contribution.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Section was first published well after the WIN-044…054 commit burst (dome 2026-03-23; section 2026-04-25 first cut per EXP integration history). Not retroactive in the post-hoc-claim sense — it is a critical analysis of dome content that already exists.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Section does not derive numerical predictions from dome geometry; it argues the dome cannot derive MOND-scale phenomena from its own geometry. Inversion of the usual derives_from_dome semantics — this section is the analytical claim THAT no such derivation is possible.

advocate_mode_pass.description: Best dome-defender argument against this section.
advocate_mode_pass.strongest_defender_line: The section concedes the dome author had an internally-coherent ΛCDM-Counter-Prediction sub-category (kernel h3), AND that applying that label consistently would have produced a 'defensible — if much weaker — claim structure' (kernel h3 closing). The closed-world h3 then argues that NO framing rescues these WINs because the bounded firmament cannot accommodate galactic-scale physics. A smart defender will quote the kernel back at us — 'even your reviewer says my framing was defensible; you're just bickering about which weaker version I should have chosen' — exactly the tension ISS-2137 tracks. Until ISS-2137 is resolved by the analyst (via either a softening edit to the kernel or a careful explanation that 'defensible-rhetorically' and 'incoherent-substantively' are not the same axis), the defender's line is a real opening.
advocate_mode_pass.why_it_still_fails: Even granting the rhetorical concession, the closed-world h3's dimensional-architecture argument is independent of how the WINs are FRAMED. The dome's spatial extent (~10^7 m) cannot host MOND-relevant systems (~10^20 m for RAR, ~10^25 m for El Gordo lensing). That is a hard physical constraint, not a labeling dispute. The defender's reframing-rescue exists only at the level of where the WINs are FILED in the dome's taxonomy; it does nothing for the substantive incompatibility. ISS-2137 is a prose-coherence issue, not a substantive-argument-failure.

cross_win_consistency_check.description: Does the section's treatment of WIN-047/048/052/054/055 match those WINs' actual verdicts and sub_rationale fields in data/wins.json?
cross_win_consistency_check.findings: VERIFIED. All five MOND-grouping WINs carry verdict='Self-Contradicted' in data/wins.json. ISS-2102 (closed 2026-05-18) confirms the sub_rationale rendering pipeline now exposes the 'category-error import (MOND grouping)' rationale on the live site for WIN-047/048/054/055; WIN-052's sub_rationale is marked 'audit-trail only' per build-scripts/generate-html.js filter, which is the documented exception. No cross-WIN inconsistency.

deeper_analysis: The c3 → c4 cycle is a clean verification round: four out of five patches landed verbatim, one (H6) landed in the explicit scope the decider documented ('kernel h3') but missed an asymmetric occurrence of the same string in the self-own h3. The H2 patch is correct but produces a stylistic redundancy. Both new c4 holes are minor and easily patched. The section's analytical core is intact: the MOND-grouping argument turns on (a) the import-vs-derivation distinction, (b) the dimensional-architecture incompatibility, and (c) the closed-world commitments — none of those were touched by the c3 patches, and the verification finds them unchanged. ISS-2135 (h1 redundancy → EXP-428) and ISS-2137 (h4 tension) are the substantive open work; this c4 round does not extend either. The decider can ship the H6/H2 minor fixes as a single small patch on the next pass.
summary_for_decider: c3 H2/H3/H5/H7 patches verified clean — applied as advertised, no new issues. H6 patch verified PARTIAL: kernel h3 cleaned, but self-own h3 paragraph 3 still contains the same '69 Confirmed Predictions' version-tied citation. The applied-patches log accurately scopes the patch as 'kernel h3' so this is not a mis-described patch — it's an incomplete one. Recommend a one-line follow-up patch to generalize the self-own h3 paragraph 3 the same way. Separately, H2 patch left a redundant WIN-052 anchor in the same h3; recommend the c3 H2 Option A wording (or just dropping the closing parenthetical). Both new holes are minor. Verdict unchanged. ISS-2135 and ISS-2137 are explicitly NOT restated as carry-over holes — they remain assigned-analyst in flight. unresolved_prior_cycle is empty by design (no carry-over restating).
